episode0.title: THE EIGHTH PASSENGER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V4

puzzle0.title: POD IDENTITY CORRELATION
puzzle0.description: Match each pod record with the correct identity clue.

event6.text: I need you to understand that I hate saying that now.

event8.text: Every time I say I found something, the universe takes it as a dare.

event1.text: A life support module. External. Still transmitting.

event13.text: At first I thought it was wreckage.

event17.text: Not a dead blink. A rhythm.

event19.text: Like someone tapping on the wall from the other side.

event22.text: Grant used to tap on bulkheads when he was nervous.

event24.text: Said it helped him 'negotiate with metal.'

event26.text: Owens said metal had better legal representation.

event31.text: I do that now. I remember something stupid so I don't have to think about the terrifying thing in front of me.

event2.text: Please tell me the universe is done being weird for one minute.

event35.text: Actually don't. I don't believe either of us would survive that much optimism.

event1.text: I like breathing. I have grown attached to it.

event2.text: But if there's someone inside, distance won't save them.

event8.text: I know what you're doing, by the way.

event10.text: Trying to keep me from rushing in.

event17.text: Noah used to say I treated caution like an insult.

event19.text: I told him caution was fine.

event1.title: POD MANIFEST
event1.content:
Occupant: UNKNOWN
Status: CRITICAL
Life Support: 23%
Signal Origin: External emergency pod
Beacon Integrity: unstable
Manifest Link: corrupted
Visual Feed: unavailable
event1.caption: Recovered emergency pod manifest. Occupant identity unavailable.

event8.text: I hate how clean those words are.

event10.text: They make panic sound like a spreadsheet.

event16.text: Please tell me I didn't just imagine that.

event19.text: Because I have imagined voices before.

event21.text: You spend long enough alone and the station starts borrowing people you miss.

event0.title: POD BEACON ORIGIN
event0.content:
Beacon Source: External emergency pod
Launch Vector: Unknown
Docking History: no confirmed ECHO dock record
Carrier Trace: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
Movement Pattern: inconsistent with passive drift
event0.caption: Recovered beacon origin report. Pod movement appears non-passive.
event0.message: [POD BEACON ORIGIN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event12.text: They drift. They tumble. They die quietly.

event19.text: Please say the engineering version of 'that's bad.'

event4.text: [NOTIFICATION QUEUED: POD LIFE SUPPORT SCAN COMPLETE]

event5.title: POD LIFE SUPPORT TRACE
event5.content:
Current Capacity: 23%
Pressure: unstable but contained
Thermal Control: failing
Occupant Biometric Signal: active
Heartbeat Pattern: irregular
Estimated Failure Window: 24 hours
External Access: possible with relay guidance
event5.caption: Life support telemetry recovered from emergency pod.
event5.message: [POD LIFE SUPPORT TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event21.text: Because he said every drill assumed everyone would become useful under pressure.

event23.text: He said most people become louder.

event1.text: Not easily. Not safely. Honestly, not in any way that makes my survival instincts happy.

event8.text: I keep saying that because if I stop, I might start thinking about what else could be inside.

event21.text: Don't make a voice. I was doing better before it had a voice.

event12.text: You don't get to use that voice on me.

event1.text: Whatever is inside that pod is not worth the risk.

event2.text: And before Maya says I sound heartless, I know exactly how I sound. That is why you should listen.

event19.text: Sometimes something looks like it needs help because that's the only shape it can use to make you open the door.

event32.text: You only get this polite when you're terrified.

event1.text: If this gets us killed, I will write a very passive-aggressive apology note in the afterlife.

event7.text: If someone is breathing, we try.

event9.text: That's the rule. Maybe the only one left.

event12.text: Noah would have said something stupid here.

event14.text: Something like, 'heroism is just panic with better lighting.'

event16.text: Then he would have grinned like he'd invented courage.

event1.text: Not a kind one. But rational.

event5.text: And since rational decisions keep being the ones that make Maya hate us, I am enjoying this less than usual.

backgroundEvent0.text: There is still someone breathing in there, Hale.

backgroundEvent7.text: Or something making a machine say they are.

backgroundEvent8.text: You know Noah hated being left waiting.

event2.text: The pod is close enough for diagnostics.

event4.text: Not close enough to touch, which is probably good because my hands are making stupid decisions without me.

event9.text: Give me something technical before I start doing something brave and idiotic.

event0.title: POD MOVEMENT ANALYSIS
event0.content:
Movement Type: active correction bursts
Thruster Signature: minimal
Guidance Source: unknown
External Pull: not detected
Conclusion: pod is adjusting course without visible command authorization
event0.caption: Recovered pod movement analysis. Beacon movement is not passive drift.
event0.message: [POD MOVEMENT ANALYSIS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event9.text: I know, I know. We are all tired of that word.

event11.text: But impossible is still the correct technical term for 'I hate this.'

event14.text: Noah used to argue with autopilot systems.

event2.title: POD SUIT IDENTIFIER
event2.content:
Recovered Suit ID: NV-08
Visual Feed: unavailable
Suit Registry Match: partial
Archive Match: Noah Voss
Identifier Integrity: 72%
Warning: suit identity may not equal occupant identity
event2.caption: Suit identifier recovered from emergency pod telemetry.
event2.message: [POD SUIT IDENTIFIER ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event10.text: He used to write his initials on everything.

event12.text: NV on tool tags. NV on cable caps. NV on ration boxes.

event14.text: Owens said it made him look like a child going to camp.

event16.text: He said it made him prepared.

event19.text: But suit identity may not equal occupant identity.

event1.text: No. Manifest unknown, suit ID NV-08, life signs active.

event4.text: Keep the records separate or the pod wins.

event6.text: I hate saying that. I hate that it sounds accurate.

event9.text: Do not turn a suit into a person just because we want a person.

event6.text: The pod remains silent for exactly three seconds.

event13.text: For a moment, all you hear is her trying not to answer.

event1.text: That suit belonged to Noah Voss.

event2.text: His psych file was missing because there was not supposed to be a file anymore.

event10.text: At least... that's what we were told.

event12.text: I am getting very tired of sentences that start that way.

event21.text: But every time the lights came back on, there he was, butchering some old Earth song like it personally owed him money.

event24.text: If that's really his voice in that pod...

event26.text: I don't know what I'll do.

event8.text: I remember the frost on the visor.

event10.text: I remember thinking no one should look that peaceful in a place like this.

event2.text: And if that thing is using his suit ID, then it is not asking for help. It is baiting us.

event14.text: Please understand how much I want to be wrong.

event25.text: That was his way of liking people.

event33.text: Most days I needed the second one more.

event0.title: DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS
event0.content:
Subject: Noah Voss
Status: deceased
Reported Date: before Incident Alpha
Body Recovery: confirmed by Engineering Lead
Witness: Ethan Hale
Conflict: emergency pod currently transmits active biometric signal under suit ID NV-08
event0.caption: Recovered death record conflicts with active pod telemetry.
event0.message: [VOSS DEATH RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event9.text: No. Don't 'Maya' me. You saw the body?

event23.text: A suit sealed badly enough that no one should have survived it.

event25.text: That is still not the same thing.

backgroundEvent2.text: Don't leave me in the dark again.

backgroundEvent4.text: You said you could hear me.

event11.text: Her breathing comes through the channel first.

event19.text: Or I know what it wants me to think I know.
THEN WHO IS INSIDE THE POD?

event5.text: Life support trace says active biometric signal.

event10.text: Don't collapse them into one just because one truth would be easier to survive.

event15.text: Do not honor him by making one now.
